lastMeeting.summary: On 2026-04-02, the Mets lost to the San Francisco Giants 2-7 at Oracle Park.
lastMeeting.recapHeadline: Susac's 3 hits in first MLB start spark cheers -- and a little crying
recapNotes: Daniel Susac ’s fiancée Drew Koltko told him heading into Thursday that he’d pick up his first Major League hit in his first at-bat of the game that night.
That’s just the cherry on top of Susac’s ice cream sundae of a ballgame, his first start behind the plate in the Major Leagues.

writeup.headline: Mets vs San Francisco Giants: starting pitching is the clearest angle
writeup.synopsis: Nolan McLean vs Tyler Mahle sets the matchup, but the strongest supported angle is starting pitching. xERA/FIP profile: Nolan McLean 2.96/4.35 vs Tyler Mahle 6.30/1.40; K-BB% 27.3 to 23.5. Several key inputs are still missing, so the read should stay conservative.

whyMetsHaveACase: Nolan McLean gives New York the cleaner run-prevention case in the underlying metrics that are actually available.
There is at least a plausible positive-regression case if the Mets' quality of contact finally turns into actual runs.
The clearest Mets path is the projected lineup carrying the better overall offensive shape and expected contact quality.

The softer context inputs lean slightly away from New York, and several of those inputs are still incomplete.
analysis.bottomLine: The board still leans Mets, but the strongest case is narrow enough that the writeup should stay disciplined.

sections.heading: 7. Projected Lineup Comparison

sections.heading: 8. Game Analysis
sections.body:
Why the Mets have a case
• Nolan McLean gives New York the cleaner run-prevention case in the underlying metrics that are actually available.
• There is at least a plausible positive-regression case if the Mets' quality of contact finally turns into actual runs.
• The clearest Mets path is the projected lineup carrying the better overall offensive shape and expected contact quality.

Where the risk is
• Bullpen support is not a clean Mets edge, especially with both relief groups carrying recent workload.
• The softer context inputs lean slightly away from New York, and several of those inputs are still incomplete.

Bottom line
The board still leans Mets, but the strongest case is narrow enough that the writeup should stay disciplined.

sections.heading: 9. Official MetsMoneyline Pick
sections.body: The best path starts with Nolan McLean giving New York the steadier underlying pitching line. There is also a reasonable positive-regression case if the Mets' contact quality finally cashes in.

writeup.headline: Mets vs San Francisco Giants: overall lineup quality is the clearest angle
writeup.synopsis: David Peterson vs Robbie Ray sets the matchup, but the strongest supported angle is overall lineup quality. Overall lineup quality only: projected WAR 0.3 vs 0.2, projected lineup wRC+ 91.1 vs 71.9, xwOBA 0.324 vs 0.258. Several key inputs are still missing, so the read should stay conservative.

sections.heading: 1. Matchup Snapshot
sections.body: David Peterson vs Robbie Ray sets the matchup, but the strongest supported angle is overall lineup quality. Overall lineup quality only: projected WAR 0.3 vs 0.2, projected lineup wRC+ 91.1 vs 71.9, xwOBA 0.324 vs 0.258. Several key inputs are still missing, so the read should stay conservative.

sections.heading: 2. Starting Pitching
sections.body: xERA/FIP profile: David Peterson 3.14/3.17 vs Robbie Ray 3.29/4.11; K-BB% 4.0 to 19.0.

sections.heading: 3. Lineup Edge
sections.body: Overall lineup quality only: projected WAR 0.3 vs 0.2, projected lineup wRC+ 91.1 vs 71.9, xwOBA 0.324 vs 0.258.

sections.heading: 4. Bullpen / Context
sections.body: Bullpen shape: xFIP 3.46 vs 3.38, WHIP 1.18 vs 1.21, last 3-day usage 27.2 IP vs 26.0 IP. Series game 1. Rest/travel: Mets 0 days, opponent 0; bullpen tax heavy/heavy.

sections.heading: 5. Why the Mets Have a Case
sections.body: The best Mets case is lineup quality: the projected group carries a small WAR edge and the stronger expected contact profile, even without true handedness-split data.

sections.heading: 6. Where the Risk Is
sections.body: The main concern is that the Mets' contact-quality indicators still have not converted into actual production, so the offense is more projection than payoff right now. Robbie Ray still owns the better K-BB profile, so the strike-throwing edge is on the other side even if the surface numbers are not.

sections.heading: 7. Analytical Lean
sections.body: Opponent. The weighted board leans to the other side, largely because New York is still carrying more real risk than clean support in the current data.

sections.heading: 8. Official MetsMoneyline Pick
sections.body: The clearest case for backing the Mets is that the projected lineup still grades better overall, especially in expected offensive quality. That said, this is one of the more self-aware Mets ML spots: the analytical read is not fully on their side, so the brand pick is leaning on the best plausible New York path rather than a clean all-in edge.

lastMeeting.summary: On 2026-03-31, the Mets lost to the St. Louis Cardinals 0-3 at Busch Stadium.
lastMeeting.recapHeadline: With Cards pitcher in a bind, Gold Glover's hose puts out the fire
recapNotes: For the second night in a row at Busch Stadium, a Cardinals starter pitched into the sixth inning but saw his outing screech to a halt before he could record an out in the frame.
Andre Pallante cruised through five innings before the heart of the Mets’ order started to get to him in the sixth.
